spec_match.rationale: Each component now has an ancestor scope host that sets anchor-scope: --menu-anchor while the descendant trigger declares anchor-name and the popover references it with position-anchor.

interactive.rationale: Two tab buttons swap ancestor scoped vs unscoped mode, menu triggers toggle popovers, and the live readout reports computed scope plus outside-observer behavior.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card now promises ancestor-scoped component wrappers that keep reused internal names inside the subtree; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo now distinguishes ancestor scoping from CSS property inheritance and includes a live outside-observer probe for anchor leakage.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout. Menu trigger / popover use design system tokens consistently.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP pre-fix verification showed the page applying anchor-scope directly to component panels and describing a collision contrast that was not reliably observable. The repaired concept adds a per-component ancestor scope host, a computed-style readout showing the wrapper carries anchor-scope while the trigger does not inherit it, an outside-observer probe, and a conformance assertion for the ancestor-wrapper contract.
